description: A HashSet is an immutable set data structure that provides efficient storage and retrieval of unique values. It uses a HashMap internally for optimal performance.
Extracts the element type from a `HashSet`. For `HashSet.HashSet<A>`, `HashSet.Value<...>` resolves to `A`.
Creates an empty HashSet.
Creates a HashSet from a variable number of values.
examples:
import { HashSet } from "effect"

const fruits = HashSet.make("apple", "banana", "cherry")
console.log(HashSet.size(fruits)) // 3

const numbers = HashSet.make(1, 2, 3, 2, 1) // Duplicates ignored
console.log(HashSet.size(numbers)) // 3

const mixed = HashSet.make("hello", 42, true)
console.log(HashSet.size(mixed)) // 3

description: Creates a HashSet from an iterable collection of values.
Checks if a value is a HashSet.
examples:
import { HashSet } from "effect"

const set = HashSet.make(1, 2, 3)
const array = [1, 2, 3]

console.log(HashSet.isHashSet(set)) // true
console.log(HashSet.isHashSet(array)) // false
console.log(HashSet.isHashSet(null)) // false

description: Adds a value to the HashSet, returning a new HashSet.
Checks if the HashSet contains the specified value.
Removes a value from the HashSet, returning a new HashSet.
Returns the number of values in the HashSet.
Checks if the HashSet is empty.
Creates the union of two HashSets.
Creates the intersection of two HashSets.
Creates the difference of two HashSets (elements in the first set that are not in the second).
Checks if a HashSet is a subset of another HashSet.
Maps each value in the HashSet using the provided function.
Filters the HashSet keeping only values that satisfy the predicate.
Tests whether at least one value in the HashSet satisfies the predicate.
Tests whether all values in the HashSet satisfy the predicate.
Reduces the HashSet to a single value by iterating through the values and applying an accumulator function.

A mutable hash set for storing unique values with Effect structural equality support. Operations mutate the set in place. Values that implement `Equal` / `Hash` can be de-duplicated structurally; other values use normal JavaScript reference or primitive equality.
Checks if the specified value is a `MutableHashSet`, `false` otherwise.
Creates an empty MutableHashSet.
examples:
import { MutableHashSet } from "effect"

const set = MutableHashSet.empty<string>()

// Add some values
MutableHashSet.add(set, "apple")
MutableHashSet.add(set, "banana")
MutableHashSet.add(set, "apple") // Duplicate, no effect

console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 2
console.log(Array.from(set)) // ["apple", "banana"]

description: Creates a MutableHashSet from an iterable collection of values. Duplicates are automatically removed.
Creates a MutableHashSet from a variable number of values. Duplicates are automatically removed.
Adds a value to the MutableHashSet, mutating the set in place. If the value already exists, the set remains unchanged.
Checks if the MutableHashSet contains the specified value.
Removes the specified value from the MutableHashSet, mutating the set in place. If the value doesn't exist, the set remains unchanged.
Returns the number of unique values in the MutableHashSet.
examples:
import { MutableHashSet } from "effect"

const set = MutableHashSet.empty<string>()
console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 0

MutableHashSet.add(set, "apple")
MutableHashSet.add(set, "banana")
MutableHashSet.add(set, "apple") // Duplicate
console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 2

MutableHashSet.remove(set, "apple")
console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 1

MutableHashSet.clear(set)
console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 0

description: Removes all values from the MutableHashSet, mutating the set in place. The set becomes empty after this operation.
examples:
import { MutableHashSet } from "effect"

const set = MutableHashSet.make("apple", "banana", "cherry")

console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 3

// Clear all values
MutableHashSet.clear(set)

console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 0
console.log(MutableHashSet.has(set, "apple")) // false
console.log(Array.from(set)) // []

// Can still add new values after clearing
MutableHashSet.add(set, "new")
console.log(MutableHashSet.size(set)) // 1
